title: Hearing Aid Specialists
description: Select and fit hearing aids for customers. Administer and interpret tests of hearing. Assess hearing instrument efficacy. Take ear impressions and prepare, design, and modify ear molds.

tasks.text: Train clients to use hearing aids or other augmentative communication devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Counsel patients and families on communication strategies and the effects of hearing loss.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and administer tests to evaluate hearing or related disabilities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Administer basic hearing tests including air conduction, bone conduction, or speech audiometry tests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain or repair hearing aids or other communication devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform basic screening procedures, such as pure tone screening, otoacoustic screening, immittance screening, and screening of ear canal status using otoscope.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create or modify impressions for earmolds and hearing aid shells.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read current literature, talk with colleagues, and participate in professional organizations or conferences to keep abreast of developments in audiology.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Demonstrate assistive listening devices (ALDs) to clients.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist audiologists in performing aural procedures, such as real ear measurements, speech audiometry, auditory brainstem responses, electronystagmography, and cochlear implant mapping.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Diagnose and treat hearing or related disabilities under the direction of an audiologist.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
